mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% as of 2018-11-15 indicates easing cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods (FEDFUNDS < 2.0%) are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment exceeding 0.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts and retail sales signals weakening aggregate demand. When both HOUST declines > 5% and RSAFS declines > 3% month-over-month simultaneously, forward equity returns compress over 20 trading days due to reduced earnings growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% AND RSAFS declines > 3% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 853 thousand and RSAFS at 421,147 million as of 2018-10-15
failure_condition: Interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rising above 100 (2017 baseline) signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains, supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.67 as of 2018-11-15, below 100 threshold
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with supply constraints easing
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns when INDPRO > 100 are not significantly higher than when INDPRO < 100 (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.0% signals labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and inflation risk, allowing the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative policy. This supports equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% as of 2018-11-15, well above 4.0% threshold
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-unemployment periods (UNRATE > 4.0%) are not significantly higher than low-unemployment periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by year-over-year CPI growth below 2.5% combined with federal funds rate above 2.0% signals monetary policy tightness relative to inflation, constraining equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 231.49 and FEDFUNDS at 0.11% as of 2018-11-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with contained inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly declines below 2.0% annualized rate signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,746.2 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2018-07-15
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-growth quarters (< 2.0% annualized) are not significantly lower than high-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST rising above 1,000 thousand units annualized signals strong residential investment demand and forward economic momentum, supporting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through accelerated earnings growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 853 thousand as of 2018-10-15, below 1,000 threshold
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns when HOUST > 1,000 are not significantly higher than when HOUST < 1,000 (t-test p > 0.10).
